==Biography==
Steve "'''Chauster'''" Chau hails from San Jose, California. The youngest of his family, Chau always followed his older siblings and mimicked whatever they did, which was predominantly playing games. He first played NES games like Contra, Double Dragon, and Tecmo Wrestling before transitioning to SNES and PlayStation. During that time, he began playing Starcraft I exclusively. However, Chau found it difficult to balance his time for gaming and education, so he decided to take a break from gaming. When he found free time, he tried out League of Legends just as something to do to pass time but eventually became hooked.

===League of Legends Career===

Throughout his career, Chauster believed that even though he didn't have the best mechanics, reaction time, or skill, he makes up for it with his superior knowledge of gameplay. That entire career was spent with [[Counter Logic Gaming]], from April 2010 to his retirement in 2013. However, throughout that career he played multiple roles on the team. Chauster started as AD Carry as one of the founding members of the team, where he  played until November 2011. He then moved to support, replacing [[Elementz]] in that role when [[Doublelift]] joined the team as starting AD Carry.

In October 2012, Chauster moved to the jungle in the starting roster, when [[Locodoco]] joined the team as support, [[Voyboy]] left, and [[HotshotGG]] moved to toplane. He stayed there until May 2013, when he switched roles once again, landing back in support with [[bigfatlp]] rejoining the team in the jungle. Chauster spent the rest of his CLG career there.

On October 23, Chauster announced his retirement from League of Legends.<ref>[http://clgaming.net/news/510-chauster-retires-from-league-of-legends-clg-roster-changes Retirement] ''clgaming.net''</ref> He was replaced by [[Aphromoo]]. In February 2014, he returned for two final games in the [[Riot_League_Championship_Series/North_America/2014_Season/Spring_Round_Robin|NA LCS Spring Split]] as a sub for CLG.

== Trivia ==
* Known for his {{ci|Ezreal}} early in his career.
* His favorite champion was at one point {{ci|Lee Sin}}<ref>http://www.progamingtours.net/index.php?/League-of-Legends/counter-logic-gaming-clg.html</ref>.
* Was known as one of the most intelligent players in the competitive scene and was often considered the brains behind CLG.
* Has played every single role in the game (Top, Jungler, Mid, AD and Support) professionally.
* Was part of two first-place teams at the World Cyber Games (2010/2011)<ref>[http://na.leagueoflegends.com/board/showthread.php?t=273471 CLG are the 2010 WCG Champions] "na.leagueoflegends.com"</ref><ref>[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B1NuhjxnjrI CDE Interview After 1st Place at WCG 2011] "youtube.com"</ref>.
* Lives in Northern California and has co-cast for the IGN Pro League.
* Has been noted to be unable to drink large amounts of alcohol <ref>[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=edcTH2UD1Fg CHAUSTER HANDLE YOUR SH*T!] ''youtube.com''</ref>.
* Has an [http://clgaming.net/forums/board3-league-of-legends/board4-general/2515-ask-me-questions/ Ask Me Anything thread] on the Clgaming.net forums that has over 25,000 posts and 6.0 Million views.
**He temporarily closed his Ask Me Anything thread after [[Bigfatlp]] stepped down to be a substitute.<ref>[http://clgaming.net/forums/board3-league-of-legends/board4-general/2515-ask-me-questions/index2160.html#post79822 ask me questions - Page 2,160] ''clgaming.net''</ref>.
* He was the main shot caller for [[Counter Logic Gaming]] while he was on the team.
* Graduated from UC Berkeley with a Dietetics major.
* Innovated the triple-teleport strategy, surprising viewers of the [[Season 2/Regional Finals - Seattle|S2 NA Regionals]].
* He  currently works as an accountant.<ref>[http://www.gamespot.com/videos/chauster-reflects-on-esports-glory-days-in-his-cur/2300-6428355/ Chauster Reflects on Esports Glory Days in His Current Life, Says Current Scene Lacks Old Personality] "gamespot.com"</ref>
* Chauster is one of only a few players in esports to have won two WCG titles, alongside the likes of Starcraft legend BoxeR and Warcraft's Grubby.
